name: Kingfisher
description: A flash of brilliant blue and orange skimming low over the water, the kingfisher is one of the most striking birds found along rivers and streams. It hunts by watching from a perch before diving with remarkable precision to catch small fish and aquatic insects. Although often elusive, its sharp call and rapid flight can reveal its presence long before it is seen.
short_description: A flash of blue and orange along the river. Often heard before it is seen.

name: Grey Wagtail
description: A small bird often seen walking quickly along shallow water and stones, flicking its long tail as it searches for insects.
short_description: A small riverside bird moving quickly along stones and shallow water.

name: Dragonflies
description: Dragonflies patrol sunny edges of still water, reed beds, and gravel bars through the warmer months.
short_description: Patrolling low over still water, reeds, and sunny gravel bars.

name: Raccoon Dog
description: A shy mammal using river corridors, woodland edges, and quiet paths, usually active around dusk or night.
short_description: A shy mammal using river corridors and woodland edges.
